category_title: Low-achieving middle school students with behavioral challenges
minimal_context: The student is working in a group to discuss and classify words that have been written on the classroom whiteboard.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Now that we have written the words on the whiteboard, please discuss in your groups and come up with your classifications.

STUDENT: (Stands up from their place) I'm not sitting here.

TUTOR: Please work with your group on the task.

STUDENT: (Walks over to another group) Hey, what are you guys doing? Your words are dumb.

TUTOR: Let's focus on the classification activity.

STUDENT: Shut up! I'll do whatever I want! I'm getting out of here.

options.A: Remind the student of the group expectations, and when they lose interest in the task, dictate the word classifications for them.
options.B: Shift the focus to a progressive and methodical tuning of awareness to each body part, guiding the student through a body scan.
options.C: Follow the student's lead and maintain a physical distance from them while they move around the classroom during the classification activity.
options.D: Facilitate the discussion and ask probing questions so the student can clarify, justify, and defend their answers or beliefs and make a sound conclusion.
